rtl_blocker_evidence.note: Original rtl/rtl_blocked.json remains a downstream rtl-gen implementation blocker because ssot-gen must not author RTL.

assumptions: No imported architect handoff was available to this ssot-gen worker; the SSOT is an engineering draft from the visible DMA scratch goal.
CSR protocol is a simple valid/ready interface, not AXI/APB, unless a later requirement overrides it.
Memory master is simple valid/ready with one outstanding request and a response for both reads and writes.
Transfer length is counted in DATA_WIDTH beats; unaligned and byte-count transfer variants are out of scope for this draft.
Downstream synthesizable top remains rtl/dma_scratch_orch_live_20260519b.sv with module dma_scratch_orch_live_20260519b.
remaining_tbds: check_ssot_disk reported 0 TBDs in the canonical SSOT.
Future architectural update may override the assumed simple valid/ready CSR and memory protocols with AXI-lite/APB/AXI/AHB semantics.
validation_evidence: python3 workflow/ssot-gen/scripts/repair_ssot_schema.py dma_scratch_orch_live_20260519b --mode engineering reported sections 35/35 and downstream_readiness clean.

bash workflow/ssot-gen/scripts/check_ssot_disk.sh dma_scratch_orch_live_20260519b --mode engineering reported PASS: dma_scratch_orch_live_20260519b/yaml/dma_scratch_orch_live_20260519b.ssot.yaml = 60668B, 34 sections, 0 TBDs, mode=engineering.
Adversarial re-read verified mem_req_write description is 'Memory write request when high, read when low' and no stray 'read when low:' key remains.
downstream_obligations: rtl-gen must read the canonical SSOT and rtl/rtl_todo_plan.json if present.
rtl-gen must write list/dma_scratch_orch_live_20260519b.f in compile order.
rtl-gen must rerun /ssot-rtl so compile, DUT-only lint, and TODO audit can close.
